messages.content:
Which statement best describes a similarity between a democracy and a republic?
￼
A.
Both guarantee their citizens the same rights.
￼
B.
Both grant the majority unlimited power.￼
C.
Both are based on the principles of feudalism.
￼
D.
Both get their sovereignty from their citizens.
D. Both get their sovereignty from their citizens.

Which diagram accurately reflects how a historical society influenced the modern U.S. government?
￼
A.
￼
￼
B.
￼￼
C.
￼
￼
D.
￼
Without the context of the historical society being referred to, it is impossible to determine which diagram accurately reflects how it influenced the modern U.S. government.

Which statement best describes a difference between presidential and parliamentary democracies?￼
A.
In a presidential democracy, there is a legislative branch but no executive branch. In a parliamentary democracy, there is an executive branch but no legislative branch.￼
B.
In a parliamentary democracy, voters are able to directly elect the head of state. In a presidential democracy, the head of government is a member of the legislature.￼
C.
In a parliamentary democracy, the head of state is a member of the legislature. In a presidential democracy, the head of state is directly elected by citizens.￼
D.
In a presidential democracy, the executive and legislative branches are joined. In a parliamentary democracy, the two branches are separate.
C. In a parliamentary democracy, the head of state is a member of the legislature. In a presidential democracy, the head of state is directly elected by citizens.

Which statement best describes a similarity between the political systems in ancient Greece and the Roman Republic?
￼
A.
Both reflected the philosophy of direct democracy.
￼
B.
Both were based on principles established during the Enlightenment.￼
C.
Both avoided centralizing political power in the hands of one person.
￼
D.
Both were designed to protect existing feudal systems.
C. Both avoided centralizing political power in the hands of one person.

Which aspect of U.S. politics is most closely tied to the concept of direct democracy?
￼
A.
Some cities allow citizens to hold town hall meetings to vote on new laws.￼
B.
The Constitution protects all citizens' basic rights to freedom of speech and religion.
￼
C.
All citizens, regardless of gender, are allowed to vote for congressional representatives.￼
D.
Every four years, elections are held to select the president of the United States.
A. Some cities allow citizens to hold town hall meetings to vote on new laws.

Which statement best summarizes the role citizens play in a democracy?
￼
A.
Citizens are the source of political sovereignty.
￼
B.
Citizens are the creators of new democratic principles.￼
C.
Citizens are the defenders of the country from foreign influence.
￼
D.
Citizens are the political representatives of noncitizens.
A. Citizens are the source of political sovereignty.

Which statement best describes an advantage of direct democracy over indirect democracy?
￼
A.
It can better ensure that political leaders are highly qualified for their jobs.
￼
B.
It can better reflect the democratic principle of majority rule.￼
C.
It can be more efficient for societies with large populations.
￼
D.
It can better prevent political leaders from serving their own interests.
B. It can better reflect the democratic principle of majority rule.

What is one of the basic principles of democracy?
￼
A.
Suppressing individual freedoms in favor of social unity
￼
B.
Avoiding compromise by following a clear ideology￼
C.
Ensuring that all citizens have equality under the law
￼
D.
Obeying majority rule even if minority groups may be harmed
C. Ensuring that all citizens have equality under the law.

ad this passage:
A democratic government allows its citizens to vote directly on most issues. However, the citizens cannot simply pass any law they want. The government has aconstitution guaranteeing that certain rights can never be denied to any citizen. These guarantees were included to make sure the most popular political parties would be unable to pass laws limiting the political rights of their opponents.
Which principle of democracy does the government described in the passage most directly represent? ￼
A.
Valuing the need to compromise
￼
B.
Respecting the equality of all citizens
￼
C.
Recognizing the worth and dignity of the individual
￼
D.
Accepting majority rule but insisting on minority rights
D. Accepting majority rule but insisting on minority rights.

A government makes its decisions based on public input. Once a month, citizens gather in a public meeting to voice their concerns, propose new policies, and debate important issues. At the end of the meeting, the people take a vote to decide new policies. Any proposal that receives a majority of the votes becomes law.The government described in the passage most reflects the features of which type of democracy?
￼
A.
Direct democracy
￼
B.
Presidential democracy
￼
C.
Parliamentary democracy
￼
D.
Indirect democracy
A. Direct democracy
